accurate.why: All claims are accurate and consistent with the bill text including reporting requirements and public availability deadline.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; all details grounded in the bill.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill as establishing a policy on appraisal services for Interior Department transactions.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Notes preexisting state/federal licensing laws no longer apply to covered transactions under this Act.

exceptions_conditions.why: Accurately covers the three conditions for using out-of-state appraisers (unavailability, qualification, cost).

effective_dates.why: Notes 90-day deadline for public availability and annual reporting starting one year after enactment.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers one-state licensing rule, preference for in-state appraisers, out-of-state conditions, reporting, and public availability.

correct_entities.why: Correctly identifies Secretary of the Interior and Department of the Interior.

correct_figures.why: Correctly states 90-day deadline and annual reporting for at least four more years after first year.

judged_via: claude-sonnet-4-6 via parallel Claude Code subagents (web/knowledge-verified context)
